headlines.text: Iran war enters Week 6: US searches for missing F-15 crew member as strikes hit Bushehr nuclear site perimeter
Iran fires 23 ballistic missiles and 56 drones at UAE in largest single-day Gulf attack of the war
Trump fires AG Pam Bondi, replaces her with his former personal criminal defense lawyer Todd Blanche
Gas hits $4.09 national average — first time above $4 since 2022, with California topping $5.89
Super Mario Galaxy Movie dominates Easter weekend box office with projected $186M+ five-day haul
Pentagon budget request hits record $1.5 trillion — largest year-over-year military spending jump since WWII
Artemis II crew completes translunar injection burn, four astronauts now en route around the moon
CNN poll: Democrats lead Republicans by 6 points on generic congressional ballot ahead of midterms
Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George and two other top generals with no explanation
Russia evacuates 198 Rosatom staff from Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant after fourth strike on the site

breaking.text: LIVE: US still searching for missing F-15 crew member in Iran; UAE intercepts 56 drones and 23 missiles in single day

stories.hed: Two US jets down, Bushehr nuclear plant struck, missing pilot still missing — your regular Saturday in the Middle East
stories.dek: The war entered its 6th week with one F-15 crew member rescued and one still missing over Iran, while US-Israeli strikes hit two petrochemical plants in Khuzestan and struck near the Bushehr nuclear site — killing a security guard and prompting Russia to evacuate 198 Rosatom employees. Iran responded by firing 23 ballistic missiles and 56 drones at the UAE in what is shaping up to be the geopolitical equivalent of a food fight at a munitions depot.

stories.hed: Iran partially opens Strait of Hormuz to 'essential goods' — but won't say what counts as essential
stories.dek: Tehran announced vessels carrying essential goods may pass through the Strait of Hormuz, which has been effectively closed since the war began, sending a slight signal of de-escalation. The catch: Iran has not defined what qualifies as 'essential,' leaving global shipping in the awkward position of guessing whether their cargo of semiconductors or soybeans makes the cut.

stories.hed: Trump fires AG Pam Bondi, installs his own former criminal defense attorney as acting Attorney General
stories.dek: President Trump ousted Pam Bondi as Attorney General and replaced her with Todd Blanche — the same lawyer who defended Trump in his New York criminal hush-money trial — in a move that legal experts described as unprecedented and Democrats described as somewhat on-brand. Bondi was praised effusively in Trump's Truth Social post, which is typically how you find out you've been fired.

stories.hed: Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George and two other generals — Pentagon offers no reason
stories.dek: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth abruptly dismissed Army Chief of Staff General Randy George along with two other senior generals, offering no public explanation for the firings. The New York Times reports George had recently clashed with Hegseth over blocked promotions for four Army officers — two of whom are Black and two are women — which in Washington is now apparently a fireable offense.

stories.hed: Democrats lead the generic ballot by 6 points — but their own favorability is 28 points underwater
stories.dek: A new CNN poll shows Democrats with a 6-point edge on the generic congressional ballot, exactly mirroring their 2018 advantage that preceded a 41-seat blue wave. The twist: Democrats are simultaneously polling 28 points underwater on party favorability, suggesting Americans are willing to vote for a party they actively dislike, which is either a damning indictment of Republicans or a very American thing to do.

markets.context: Markets closed flat for Good Friday as a blowout March jobs report (178K jobs added) and surging oil above $111/barrel from the Iran war kept the Fed on hold and investors on edge.

gas.note: The Strait of Hormuz is closed, your wallet is open, and the two are directly related.

popCulture.hed: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is dominating Easter weekend with a projected $186M+ five-day domestic opening
popCulture.dek: The Illumination and Universal sequel opened to $59.1M in its first two days domestically, pacing slightly ahead of the original 2023 film that went on to earn $1.36 billion globally. Critics gave it a 42% on Rotten Tomatoes while audiences handed it a 90% — a gap so wide it has its own Yoshi.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok trend using a clip of Jon Hamm losing himself on a dance floor from the Apple TV+ show 'Your Friends and Neighbors' Season 2, which dropped April 3. Creators overlay the looping dance clip — with transparency lowered for a morph effect — onto mundane personal wins or nostalgic moments, turning everyday mediocrity into an unexpected triumph.
genZ.usage: POV: You check gas prices, see $4.09 nationally, and remember Oklahoma exists at $3.27. *Jon Hamm dance overlay.*

tldr: The US-Iran war entered its sixth week on a busy Saturday, with a missing F-15 pilot, strikes near a nuclear plant, UAE hit by a drone-and-missile barrage, and Iran partially cracking open the Strait of Hormuz — all while Trump fired his Attorney General and replaced her with his old criminal defense lawyer, gas hit $4.09 a gallon, and Mario made $188 million at the movies because people needed to cope somehow.
